items.title: Random variables and PMF/PDF
items.description: Distributions are always distributions of random variables; discrete vs continuous type determines whether you write a PMF or PDF.

items.title: Gaussian Naive Bayes
items.description: Gaussian NB assumes each feature is conditionally Normal within each class, then multiplies class-conditional PDFs (with independence) for classification.

items.title: Maximum likelihood estimation
items.description: Choosing Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Categorical, or Gaussian likelihoods and maximizing log-likelihood yields MLE parameters (e.g. sample mean for Gaussian μ).

items.title: Loss functions and cross-entropy
items.description: Binary/categorical cross-entropy is negative log-likelihood under Bernoulli and Categorical models; MSE aligns with Gaussian noise assumptions in regression.
